title: Pelagodendron congestum
taxonomy: Pelagodendron congestum is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae. It is endemic to the island of Seram in Indonesia, specifically growing in the forests of the island. The species is classified as an evergreen tree and belongs to the order Malvales. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, including its leaf structure and flower arrangement. Further studies on this species are necessary to understand its full classification and relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Pelagodendron congestum, also known as Congested Pelagodendron, is a plant species that belongs to the family Dipterocarpaceae. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and protecting cells from oxidative damage. The bioactive constituents of Pelagodendron congestum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
